gaps.before: The company had invested heavily in the new factory;
gaps.after: , demand for the product collapsed within a year.
gaps.explanation: 「多額を投資した → にもかかわらず需要が急減した」という逆接。逆接を示す nevertheless が適切。

gaps.before: Had the engineers checked the sensor earlier,
gaps.explanation: Had + 主語 ... は仮定法過去完了の倒置。帰結節は would/could have + 過去分詞。could have avoided が正しい。

gaps.after: light on a question that had puzzled researchers for decades.
gaps.explanation: shed light on で「～を解明する・明らかにする」。light と結びつく動詞は shed。

readingQuestions.prompt: 文脈に最も合う選択肢を選んでください。

gaps.after: the beach clean-up until the storm had passed.
gaps.explanation: put off で「～を延期する」。嵐が過ぎるまで延期する文意に合う句動詞。

gaps.before: This is the first time I
gaps.after: a meal entirely on my own, and I feel proud of it.
gaps.explanation: It is the first time (that) 主語 have + 過去分詞 の形。現在完了 have cooked が正しい。

gaps.before: The teacher asked the students to
gaps.after: attention to the small details in the diagram.
gaps.explanation: pay attention to で「～に注意を払う」。attention と結びつく動詞は pay。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Slow Journey of a Cup of Coffee
paragraphs: Few people pause to think about how far a cup of coffee has travelled before it reaches their table. The story often begins on a small farm high in the mountains of Central America or East Africa, where the climate is cool and the soil is rich. There, ripe red coffee cherries are picked by hand, one by one, because a single branch may hold fruit at very different stages of ripeness. Machines cannot yet match the careful eye of an experienced picker.

Once the cherries are gathered, the beans hidden inside must be removed, dried, and sorted, a process that can take several weeks. Only then are the green beans shipped across oceans to distant roasting houses, where intense heat finally unlocks the rich aromas we recognise. A journey that started with a farmer bending over a bush can pass through a dozen pairs of hands before the coffee is poured into a paper cup on a busy street.

Understanding this long chain can change the way we drink. When we know that our morning coffee depends on the labour of growers who often earn very little, a cheap cup no longer seems quite so simple. A growing number of buyers now look for beans that are traded fairly, hoping that the farmers at the very start of the journey receive a reward that matches the value of their work.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: A cup of coffee passes through a long, labour-intensive journey, and knowing this can make us value the farmers behind it.
Coffee is now grown and packaged entirely by machines, so no human labour is involved.
Coffee cherries are always green and are best picked before they ripen.
Roasting has no effect on the smell or taste of coffee beans.

questions.after: because one branch can hold fruit at different stages of ripeness.

questions.prompt: コーヒーが多くの人の手を経ることを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the climate is cool and the soil is rich
intense heat finally unlocks the rich aromas we recognise
can pass through a dozen pairs of hands before the coffee is poured
a cheap cup no longer seems quite so simple

questions.sentence: This is one reason why the harvest still relies so heavily on human workers.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で筆者が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Knowing how hard growers work, and how little they often earn, is leading more buyers to seek fairly traded coffee.
Coffee should always be as cheap as possible, whatever the cost to farmers.
Farmers at the start of the journey are the only people who ever touch the beans.
Fair trade has made no difference to how people think about coffee.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: Why We Forget, and Why That Helps
paragraphs: Most of us think of forgetting as a failure of the brain, an annoying flaw that makes us lose keys and miss appointments. For a long time, scientists shared this view and studied memory mainly by asking how we manage to hold on to information. In recent years, however, researchers have begun to argue that forgetting is not simply a breakdown. Instead, it may be a useful skill that the brain has developed on purpose.

The reason is that we are flooded with new information every single day. If we remembered every face, number, and conversation with equal strength, our minds would soon become crowded and slow. By letting go of details that no longer matter, the brain keeps the memories that are truly useful easy to reach. In this sense, a good memory may depend just as much on skilful forgetting as on careful storing.

This idea offers some comfort to anyone frustrated by a poor memory. Forgetting a stranger's name or an old phone number does not mean the brain is failing; it may simply be doing its job of clearing away clutter. What matters, the researchers suggest, is not remembering everything, but keeping the right things within reach when we truly need them.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Forgetting may be a useful skill that clears away unneeded details so that important memories stay easy to reach.
Forgetting is always a sign that the brain is seriously damaged.
The brain is able to store every experience with perfect and equal strength.
Scientists have proven that memory has no purpose at all.

questions.before: By letting go of details that no longer matter, the brain keeps useful memories

questions.prompt: なぜすべてを覚えていると困るのかを説明している語句はどれですか。

spans.text: an annoying flaw that makes us lose keys and miss appointments
our minds would soon become crowded and slow
forgetting is not simply a breakdown
keeping the right things within reach when we truly need them

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Rather than a failure, forgetting may be the brain's way of removing clutter so the memories that matter stay useful.
People with poor memories should worry that their brains are broken.
The brain should try to store absolutely every detail it ever meets.
Remembering and forgetting have nothing to do with each other.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Would you mind sending me the updated schedule before you leave the office today?
The train leaves at a quarter to eight, so we should get there by seven thirty.
Little did we know that the small decision would change the entire course of the project.
I would have joined you for lunch if I had known you were in town.
The brave brown bear brushed past the bushes beside the bright blue river.
Please remember to turn off the lights and lock the door when you leave.
